flashcards.front: What is the smallest deployable unit in Kubernetes?
flashcards.back: A Pod. It can run one or more containers that share network (localhost) and volumes. Pods are ephemeral.

flashcards.front: Generate Pod YAML without creating the resource (imperative → declarative workflow).
Scale a Deployment named web to 4 replicas.
Name the three core Service types and when you use ClusterIP.
flashcards.back: ClusterIP (internal only), NodePort (open port on each node), LoadBalancer (cloud LB). ClusterIP is the default for in-cluster access.
Whether the Pod receives traffic from a Service. Failing readiness removes the Pod from Service endpoints until it passes again.

flashcards.front: Which Ansible modules configure LVM storage (partitions, VG, LV, FS, mount)?
flashcards.back: parted, lvg, lvol, filesystem, mount — in that order. Swap has no module; use command/shell.

flashcards.front: Does the mount module create the mount point directory?
flashcards.back: No. Create the directory first (file module with state=directory), then mount.

flashcards.front: Create an ext4 filesystem on logical volume /dev/vg0/lv_app and mount it at /app persistently.
flashcards.back:
mkfs.ext4 /dev/vg0/lv_app
mkdir -p /app
echo '/dev/vg0/lv_app /app ext4 defaults 0 0' >> /etc/fstab
mount -a

quizzes.question: Which Pod restartPolicy keeps a Pod running after the container exits successfully?
quizzes.explanation: Never does not restart the Pod when the container exits. Always restarts regardless of exit code; OnFailure only restarts on failure.

quizzes.question: What is the main purpose of an Ingress resource?
options: Expose HTTP/S routes to Services from outside the cluster
quizzes.explanation: Ingress defines HTTP/S routing rules to backend Services, usually via an Ingress controller.

quizzes.question: How do you run a playbook that uses Ansible Vault-encrypted variables?
quizzes.explanation: Use --ask-vault-pass or --vault-password-file so Ansible can decrypt vault blocks at runtime.

quizzes.question: Permanently open TCP port 443 with firewalld.
quizzes.explanation: Changes must use --permanent and firewalld must be reloaded for the runtime configuration to persist.

steps.text: Create a Pod named webapp using nginx:alpine.

labs.title: Configure LVM with Ansible modules
labs.summary: Ordered storage tasks using parted, lvg, lvol, filesystem, and mount.

steps.text: Create the volume group with lvg.
Create the logical volume with lvol.
Create the filesystem with filesystem module.
Create the mount point directory, then mount persistently.
